title: primula elatior subsp. poloninensis
intro: Primula elatior subsp. poloninensis, also known as the tall primrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Primulaceae, characterized by its tall stems and showy yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits associated with Primula elatior subsp. poloninensis include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, and its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including colds, fever, and digestive issues.
